seoTitle: Build a Professional Feedback Form in Microsoft Forms
metaDescription: Design a customer or employee feedback survey in Microsoft Forms using rating scales, Likert grids, NPS, and a polished visual theme.
intro: A cluttered or overly long feedback survey drives away the honest responses you actually need. This guide walks through building a clean feedback form in Microsoft Forms that mixes quantitative ratings with open text, then styling it so it looks professional on any device.

steps.heading: Start a new form
steps.body: Click New Form from your dashboard and give it a clear, descriptive title with a short set of completion instructions.

steps.heading: Add core rating fields
steps.body: Use Choice, Rating, and Long Answer text questions to combine multiple-choice demographics, star ratings, and open-ended comments.

steps.heading: Build a Likert grid for detailed evaluation
steps.body: Select the Likert question type from the extended options menu to create a multi-row scale for evaluating several criteria at once.

steps.heading: Add a Net Promoter Score question
steps.body: Drop in the NPS question type to instantly capture a standard 0-to-10 customer loyalty score.

steps.heading: Style and preview
steps.body: Use the Style panel to apply a theme with accent colors that match your brand, then use Preview to confirm the layout works on both desktop and mobile.

faq.question: What question types work best for customer feedback?
faq.answer: A mix of Rating, Likert, NPS, and Long Answer text questions balances quick quantitative data with qualitative detail.

faq.question: Can I see feedback results as charts automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, Forms generates real-time response charts and can export the underlying data to an Excel spreadsheet.

faq.question: How do I keep a feedback form from feeling too long?
faq.answer: Stick to a small set of focused questions and use sections to break longer surveys into manageable pages.

faq.question: Can I customize the visual theme to match my brand?
faq.answer: Yes, the Style panel lets you apply high-contrast themes and custom accent colors to the entire form.
